title: ESP Subcategory Failed (Certificates)
description: Detects when the Certificates subcategory of the Enrollment Status Page reported a failure, even if the enrollment later appears to have succeeded. Users can click 'Continue anyway' on an ESP error, which masks the underlying certificate provisioning failure — this rule surfaces the transition so it stays visible.
author: Autopilot Monitor
explanation:
The ESP Certificates subcategory transitioned to **failed** during enrollment. This is frequently masked when the user clicks 'Continue anyway' on the ESP error screen — the overall enrollment then appears successful while a required certificate (e.g. Wi-Fi, VPN, SCEP/PKCS) was never deployed.

The subcategory name is read from the registry (`CertificatesSubcategory`) and is language-invariant, so this rule fires regardless of OS display language.

remediation.title: Validate certificate delivery on the device
steps: Run `certutil -store My` and `certutil -store -user My` on the device to inspect installed certificates
Check `Event Viewer → Applications and Services Logs → Microsoft → Windows → DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider → Admin` for SCEP/PKCS errors
Review the Intune portal for the device's certificate profile assignments and deployment status

remediation.title: Fix common certificate provisioning issues
steps: Verify the NDES/SCEP connector is online and healthy
Confirm the certificate template permissions grant 'Enroll' to the Intune service account / NDES computer account
Check that the device has network line-of-sight to the SCEP endpoint at the time of ESP
Re-sync the device (Settings → Accounts → Access work or school → Info → Sync) and re-check the Certificates subcategory

relatedDocs.title: Microsoft Learn: Troubleshoot certificate profiles in Intune
